data.body:
PASS-1 review (T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety [overlay]): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS, no findings.

Reviewed the workflow Pass-2-verify remediation plan (WS1 drift-refresh migration; WS2 per-step `model:` key + editor/BPMN round-trip; WS3 token-based chunking; WS4 coach-call gate). T11 fires only when a plan changes a schema/persisted format or backfills data; none of T11's migration-EXECUTION checks engage here.

affirmations:
- [T11] Correctly not-applicable per the overlay's ANTI-FP rule ("not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes"). WS1 (drift-refresh onto the workflow engine + deletion of `_run_passes`/`pass2_verify`/`verifier_cfg`), WS3 (token-based verify chunking via `sizing.py`), and WS4 (gating the `coach_notes` step) are code-path / in-memory reorganization with no persisted-format or data-backfill surface. Consistent with rebar's house meaning of "migration" (code-substrate reorganization, not data migration), the same distinction prior reviewers drew on this epic's workflow-engine work.
- [T11] WS2's per-step `model:` is an ADDITIVE, optional field on git-tracked config-as-code, not a backfill of a persisted data store. The workflow IR (the source of truth) is version-controlled text, and the BPMN serialization is EPHEMERAL — bpmn.py:30-32 states "the IR is the source of truth, the visual format is never committed." There is no large persisted table, no blocking DDL, no row backfill.
- [T11] The plan's WS2 BPMN back-compat clause IS the expand/forward-compatible posture done correctly for an additive format field: "import of a pre-existing BPMN document that lacks the per-step model attribute must default gracefully to 'no per-step override' (… falls back to the workflow/cfg model via `resolve_model`), never error — legacy exports stay importable" (WS2 + AC "Importing a legacy BPMN document that lacks the per-step model attribute succeeds and falls back to the workflow/cfg model"). Because the affected artifacts are version-controlled text with full git rollback and the missing attribute defaults rather than requiring a rewrite, none of T11's execution-safety binary checks apply — online expand-contract DDL, batched/throttled backfill, resumability of a partial backfill, dual-write window, and partial-failure data loss are all not-engaged. No backfill of existing workflow files is required (legacy files simply read as "no override").

No T11 checklist items emitted for this chunk.
